title: Probability, Counting, Expected Value, and Risk · Decision Notebook: Expected Value
summary: Compare warranties, games, reliability claims, and scheduling risks with transparent assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg expected value increment in a practical risk and expected-value brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute and interpret expected value without claiming it predicts a single outcome.
Use tables, trees, multiplication, permutations, or combinations according to order and replacement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Calculate Expected Value evidence
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $30 with probability 0.30 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 30 × 0.30 + 0 × 0.70.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 30 × 0.30 + 0 × 0.70.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend the Expected Value model
